title: 10 best herbal essential oils for dizziness
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for dizziness are a natural remedy that can provide relief from the discomforting symptoms of vertigo, lightheadedness, and balance issues. These oils, extracted from plants such as peppermint, ginger, and lavender, help to calm the mind and body, reducing feelings of disorientation and nausea. For example, peppermint oil's invigorating properties can help alleviate dizziness by improving circulation and mental clarity, while ginger oil's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe digestive issues that may contribute to dizziness. By using these oils in a diffuser or applying them topically, individuals can find relief from dizziness and improve their overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with dizziness because its calming properties can help to alleviate feelings of vertigo and nausea. The oil's soothing effects on the nervous system can also help to stabilize blood pressure, which is often a contributing factor to episodes of dizziness. Additionally, lemon balm's natural antispasmodic properties may help to relax the muscles in the inner ear, which can become irritated or inflamed and contribute to feelings of lightheadedness. By using lemon balm essential oils, individuals may find relief from dizziness and improve their overall sense of balance and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Herbal lemon balm essential oil helps to relieve muscle spasms and cramps, which can contribute to dizziness by calming and relaxing the muscles, allowing for better blood flow and oxygenation.
Sedative property: The sedative properties of herbal lemon balm essential oil help to reduce anxiety and stress, common causes of dizziness, by promoting relaxation and reducing mental chatter.

Antihistamine property: Herbal lemon balm essential oil has antihistamine effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, vertigo, and other conditions that cause dizziness by reducing histamine levels in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to be effective in reducing dizziness and nausea.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves contain a high concentration of citral, a natural anti-inflammatory that can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves at dawn for maximum oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves using shade drying or air circulation for 7 days.
Steam distill the dried melissa officinalis leaves in a still at 100-150°f temperature.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a glass container and filter through coffee filters.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with dizziness because of its calming and soothing properties. When inhaled, peppermint oil's menthol compound can help to relax the digestive system and ease nausea, which is often a contributing factor to dizziness. Additionally, the oil's refreshing scent can help to clear the mind and reduce feelings of fatigue, allowing for greater balance and stability. This natural remedy can be particularly effective in helping to alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness, vertigo, and other forms of dizziness.
remedy_properties: Digestive relief: Peppermint oil's ability to soothe digestive issues, such as nausea and stomach upset, can help alleviate dizziness caused by gastrointestinal problems. This is because peppermint's menthol compound relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract and reduces inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce swelling in the inner ear, which is a common cause of dizziness and vertigo. By reducing inflammation, peppermint oil may help alleviate symptoms such as lightheadedness and balance problems.

Vasodilatory action: Peppermint oil's ability to dilate blood vessels can help increase blood flow to the brain, which can help reduce feelings of dizziness and lightheadedness caused by poor circulation or low blood pressure. This increased blood flow may also help improve cognitive function and memory.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots contain a high concentration of menthol, which has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate dizziness.
Leaves: The leaves of the peppermint plant have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help to reduce symptoms of dizziness caused by gastrointestinal issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality peppermint leaves from organic gardens in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Wash the harvested peppermint leaves gently with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steam distill 100g of cleaned peppermint leaves with 500ml of pure water for 30 minutes to extract the essential oil.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper or a glass wool filter to remove impurities.
Transfer the filtered essential oil into dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dry place for 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with dizziness because of its natural properties that work to calm the digestive system, reduce nausea, and promote circulation. When used aromatically or topically, ginger oil stimulates the body's natural response to balance and regulate blood pressure, helping to alleviate feelings of dizziness and lightheadedness. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe and relax the muscles, further contributing to its effectiveness in reducing symptoms of dizziness.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Ginger essential oil can help alleviate dizziness by stimulating digestive processes, which can reduce nausea and discomfort often associated with lightheadedness.

Anti-Inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger essential oil can help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing the sensation of dizziness and promoting a sense of balance.

Vasodilator: As a natural vasodilator, ginger essential oil can help to widen blood vessels, increasing blood flow and oxygenation to the brain, which can help to alleviate dizziness and improve overall cognitive function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ginger plant contain a high concentration of active compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, which help to alleviate dizziness by soothing the digestive system.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of ginger are rich in essential oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating dizziness caused by inner ear infections or balance disorders.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes at dawn or dusk when the fragrance is strongest.
Wash the harvested rhizomes thoroughly in warm water to remove dirt and impurities.
Slice the cleaned rhizomes into thin strips to facilitate steam distillation extraction.
Steam distill the sliced rhizome strips for 30-45 minutes using a clevenger apparatus or similar equipment.
Collect and filter the extracted essential oil, discarding any sediment and storing it in dark glass containers at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with dizziness because of its natural calming properties, which help to soothe the mind and body. The oil's aroma is known to promote feelings of relaxation and tranquility, reducing anxiety and stress that can often contribute to episodes of dizziness. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum's antispasmodic properties can help to ease muscle tension, further alleviating symptoms of dizziness. By promoting overall calmness and relaxation, this essential oil can provide relief from dizzy spells and promote a sense of balance and stability.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to feelings of dizziness and vertigo by neutralizing free radicals that can damage cells and tissues.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil may help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms of dizziness and vertigo caused by conditions such as labyrinthitis or benign paroxysmal positional vertigo (BPPV).

Stimulating and clarifying properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has a stimulating effect on the mind and emotions, which can help to clear mental fog and reduce feelings of dizziness and disorientation caused by anxiety, stress, or fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which help calm the nervous system and alleviate dizziness.
Barks: Contains a high concentration of terpenes, specifically caryophyllene oxide, known for its anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce symptoms of vertigo.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves, rinsing them thoroughly with clean water.
Steam distill the leaves using a glass still and 500 milliliters of distilled water for three hours.
Separate the essential oil from the water using a separating funnel and filter papers.
Transfer the essential oil to a dark glass bottle filled with 100 milliliters of jojoba carrier oil and store in a cool place.
Allow the mixture to mature for two weeks before use, shaking the bottle daily to ensure proper blending.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with dizziness because of its calming and stabilizing properties. Lavender oil has been shown to promote relaxation, ease anxiety, and reduce stress levels, all of which can contribute to feelings of dizziness. Additionally, lavender's gentle sedative effects can help to regulate the body's internal balance, alleviating symptoms such as lightheadedness and vertigo. By promoting a sense of calm and tranquility, English lavender essential oils can provide relief from dizziness and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Calming and Sedative Properties: English lavender oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to alleviate feelings of dizziness by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.

Antihistamine and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Lavender oil's antihistamine properties can help to reduce the histamine response that can contribute to dizziness, while its anti-inflammatory properties may help to ease tension headaches and other conditions that can cause dizziness.

Vasodilatory Properties: English lavender oil has been shown to dilate blood vessels, which can help to improve blood flow to the brain and reduce symptoms of dizziness, such as lightheadedness or vertigo.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in calcium and magnesium, which helps to calm the nervous system and alleviate symptoms of dizziness.
Leaves: The leaves contain a high concentration of linalool, a natural sedative that can help to reduce feelings of dizziness and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers from your garden or local nursery weighing approximately one kilogram.
Dry the lavender flowers in a well-ventilated area for seven to ten days ensuring optimal air circulation.
Place the dried lavender flowers into a clean glass jar and add 250 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for fourteen to twenty-one days allowing the essential oil to infuse slowly.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding the solids and transferring the oil to a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian essential oils helps with dizziness because of its natural sedative properties, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce feelings of lightheadedness. The oil's ability to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety also plays a significant role in alleviating dizziness symptoms. Additionally, valerian essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and balance the digestive system, which is often linked to feelings of dizziness. By using valerian essential oils, individuals can find natural relief from dizziness and restore their sense of balance and equilibrium.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Anxiolytic Properties: Valerian essential oil has been shown to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness by calming the nervous system.

Antihistamine and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Valerian essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which is a common cause of dizziness, as well as antihistamine properties that can help soothe irritated nerves.

Vasodilatory Properties: Valerian essential oil can help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure, which can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness caused by poor circulation or orthostatic hypotension.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the valerian plant contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, which is responsible for its sedative and calming properties, making it effective in treating dizziness.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the valerian plant have a high content of sesquiterpenes, which are known for their anti-anxiety and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate symptoms of dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots at dawn on a dry day when the plant is in full bloom.
Clean and dry the harvested roots by gently brushing off dirt and debris for 10 minutes.
Steam distill the dried valeriana officinalis roots using a clevenger apparatus for 4 hours.
Combine the essential oil extract with a carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil in a 1:10 ratio for 30 minutes.
Store the final blend in dark glass bottles at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon essential oils helps with dizziness because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm the digestive system and reduce nausea. The oil's warming and comforting aroma also stimulates circulation, promoting blood flow to the brain and helping to alleviate feelings of lightheadedness. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon essential oil has a natural sedative effect, which can help to ease anxiety and promote relaxation, further reducing the likelihood of dizziness. Overall, incorporating this oil into one's daily routine may provide relief from dizziness-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine-like properties: Cinnamon has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antihistamine-like effects, which may help alleviate dizziness caused by histamine release or allergic reactions.

Digestive relief: Cinnamon is known for its carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe digestive issues such as nausea, bloating, and discomfort that may contribute to feelings of dizziness.
Antioxidant activity: Cinnamon is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation that may exacerbate dizziness or lightheadedness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots of the Ceylon cinnamon plant are used to create a calming essential oil that can help alleviate dizziness and nausea.
Rhyzomes: With their unique chemical composition, rhyzomes of the plant have been shown to possess antispasmodic properties, making them effective in reducing symptoms of dizziness and vertigo.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cinnamomum verum leaves from sustainably managed plantations in optimal weather conditions.
Dry the harvested leaves using a gentle heat method at 60°c for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Use a solvent-free mechanical process to distill the dried leaves into a crude oil with a yield of 5-7%.
Filter the crude oil through activated charcoal and then through a diatomaceous earth filter to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the final essential oil in dark glass containers at room temperature for up to 1 year, using a minimum of 2% cinnamic aldehyde content as quality control measure.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with dizziness because it has a calming effect on the digestive system, which is often linked to dizziness and lightheadedness. The oil's natural antispasmodic properties help to soothe and relax the stomach muscles, reducing nausea and discomfort that can contribute to feelings of dizziness. Additionally, fennel essential oil's ability to stimulate digestion and reduce bloating also helps to alleviate symptoms of vertigo and dizziness, promoting a sense of balance and well-being.
remedy_properties: Carminative and anti-inflammatory: Fennel essential oil's carminative and anti-inflammatory properties help to relax digestive muscles, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to feelings of dizziness.

Stomachic and antispasmodic: The stomachic property of fennel essential oil helps to stimulate digestion and relieve nausea, while its antispasmodic property calms muscle contractions in the stomach and intestines, reducing the likelihood of dizziness-inducing digestive discomfort.

Anxiolytic and sedative: Fennel essential oil's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative properties help to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and panic that can exacerbate dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are often used because they contain a high concentration of essential oil that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and sedative properties, making them effective in relieving symptoms of dizziness.
Buds: Fennel buds are used due to their delicate flavor and aroma, which are believed to help calm the nervous system and alleviate feelings of dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves in the morning for optimal oil content.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves using paper towels and air circulation for 2 hours.
Steam distill the dried leaves at a temperature of 100°c with 10 grams per 100 milliliters water for 4 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through activated charcoal and a coffee filter for 30 minutes.
Store the final foeniculum vulgare essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesame essential oils helps with dizziness because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce feelings of vertigo and lightheadedness. The oil's antispasmodic properties can also help to alleviate any muscle spasms or tension that may be contributing to the dizziness. Additionally, sesame essential oils' ability to improve circulation can help to increase blood flow to the brain, which can help to reduce symptoms of dizziness and improve overall balance and coordination.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sesame seeds contain antioxidants like vitamin E and sesaminol, which can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to dizziness.
Vascular relaxation properties: Sesame seed extracts have been shown to relax blood vessels, which may help improve blood flow to the brain and alleviate symptoms of dizziness.

Anti-anxiety properties: Sesame seeds contain a compound called sesamin, which has been studied for its potential anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects. Reducing anxiety can also help alleviate dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesamin, a compound known to alleviate dizziness and nausea.
Stems: Contain sesamol, which has been shown to reduce symptoms of vertigo and lightheadedness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sesamum indicum leaves and flowers at dawn to ensure maximum oil content.
Clean the harvested plant material thoroughly with distilled water to remove impurities and debris.
Steam distill the cleaned sesamum indicum material using a clevenger apparatus for 2 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a clean glass vial, filtering it through paper filter if necessary.
Store the final product in dark-tinted glass bottles with tight-fitting caps, labeling them with date and contents.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric essential oils helps with dizziness because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed or applied topically, turmeric oil's active compound curcumin helps to reduce inflammation in the body, which is a common underlying cause of dizziness. Additionally, turmeric oil's warming and calming effects can help to soothe the digestive system and nervous system, both of which are closely linked to balance and equilibrium. By addressing these root causes, turmeric essential oils can provide natural relief from dizziness and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the body, which can be a contributing factor to dizziness and vertigo.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of turmeric essential oil help to neutralize free radicals in the body that can cause oxidative stress, leading to dizziness and other symptoms of vertigo.

Cyclooxygenase inhibitor: Turmeric essential oil's ability to inhibit cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes helps to reduce inflammation and pain associated with vestibular disorders, such as benign paroxysmal positional vertigo (BPPV), which can cause dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate dizziness and balance body equilibrium.
Rhyzomes: Contains sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have sedative properties, calming the nervous system and alleviating symptoms of dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh turmeric root and wash it thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities.
Dry the turmeric root in a single layer on paper towels or cloth for 7-10 hours to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried turmeric root using a steam distillation apparatus at 100°c for 4-6 hours to extract essential oil.
Combine the collected essential oil with 20ml of carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil and store it in dark glass bottles.
Allow the blended oils to mature for 7 days before use to allow the flavors and aromas to blend and stabilize.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dizziness with herbal essential oils is by using a blend of soothing and calming oils. Combine 2 drops of lavender oil, 1 drop of peppermint oil, and 1 drop of ginger oil in a diffuser or apply topically diluted with a carrier oil. Lavender calms the nervous system, peppermint helps to clear the mind, and ginger reduces nausea and dizziness.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dizziness that are treated with herbal essential oils are vertigo, lightheadedness, and anxiety. Geranium oil is often used to calm and balance the nervous system, relieving symptoms of vertigo and lightheadedness. Lavender oil has a sedative effect, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. Peppermint oil can help alleviate feelings of dizziness and nausea by calming the digestive system.
